user_query:
I’m preparing a clean, portable deployment bundle for my conference paper and talk. Using only the files under input/, please assemble a reproducible dist/ folder and generate verification artifacts so I can hand this off to the conference A/V team and our departmental repository.

What I need you to do:
- Inspect the project tree under input/ and read these files:
  - input/paper.tex (LaTeX paper)
  - input/refs.bib (bibliography)
  - input/slides/outline.md (talk outline)
  - input/metadata.yaml (project metadata)
  - input/images/ (figures)
- Extract from input/paper.tex:
  - All image file references used with \includegraphics (handle both forms with and without optional [..] arguments). Resolve them as project-relative paths.
  - All citation keys appearing in any \cite{...} commands (support comma-separated keys).
- Extract from input/slides/outline.md:
  - All image references of the form ![...](path). Resolve relative paths based on the file’s directory.
- Compare the citation keys from paper.tex against entries present in input/refs.bib (BibTeX keys). Identify which cited keys are missing from the bibliography.
- Check which referenced images actually exist under input/.

Then build a minimal deployment bundle:
- Create dist/ at the workspace root. Copy into it only the essentials:
  - dist/paper.tex (from input/paper.tex)
  - dist/refs.bib (from input/refs.bib)
  - dist/slides/outline.md (from input/slides/outline.md)
  - dist/images/ containing only the images that are actually referenced in either the paper or slides and that exist under input/ (do not copy unreferenced or missing images)
- Do not modify the contents of copied files.

Produce two verification artifacts in dist/:
1) dist/manifest.json (UTF-8 JSON) with the following fields:
   - metadata: include all keys from input/metadata.yaml (at least title, author, conference, session, artifact_version)
   - paper_citations: sorted unique list of citation keys found in paper.tex
   - missing_citations: sorted list of citation keys cited in paper.tex but not present in refs.bib
   - bibliography_size: integer count of BibTeX entries in refs.bib
   - figures_referenced: sorted unique list of all resolved figure paths referenced in paper.tex and slides (normalized to forward slashes, project-relative like "images/fig1.png")
   - figures_missing: sorted list of referenced figure paths that do not exist under input/
   - files_copied: sorted list of relative paths within dist/ for every file you copied (e.g., "paper.tex", "refs.bib", "slides/outline.md", "images/fig1.png")
   - file_sizes: object mapping each path in files_copied to its size in bytes in dist/
   - generated_at: an ISO 8601 timestamp string
2) dist/deployment_status.md with a concise status summary that I can paste into an email update. Include:
   - A one-paragraph overview stating the paper title and conference from metadata.yaml
   - A “Readiness” section with counts: total citations, missing citations, bibliography_size, total figures referenced (paper + slides), and figures_missing
   - If there are any missing items, include a short “Issues” bullet list naming missing citation keys and missing figures (using project-relative paths). Otherwise, state that the bundle is ready.

Notes and constraints:
- Resolve relative image paths based on the location of the file in which they appear (e.g., paths in slides/outline.md are relative to input/slides/).
- Normalize all paths in the JSON lists to use forward slashes and be relative to the project root (e.g., "images/fig2.jpg").
- Do not attempt to compile LaTeX or download anything. This task must be fully local and self-contained.
- Deliverables must be exactly: the dist/ folder with the copied files as specified, plus dist/manifest.json and dist/deployment_status.md. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
